essential_learning_outcomes.elo_description: To understand the diversity of the world's population is its strength
specific_curriculum_outcomes: Why is regional interaction important?
How do common sports, culture and music activities foster regional interactions?
Focus Questions:
Appreciate the value of arts and sports for shaping regional identity and pride
Investigate how local sport, culture or music figures have influence beyond their home state
Describe the common sport, culture and music activities that foster regional interactions
Learners would be able to:
How have East Indians influenced Caribbean culture?
Why were East Indians brought to the Caribbean?
Focus Questions:
Discuss the impact of East Indian culture on Caribbean society.
Understand the journey taken by East Indian Indentured Servants to the Caribbean.
Identify the reasons for the migration of East Indians to the Caribbean.
Students would be able to:
Grade 6 Social Studies Curriculum
How did slavery come to an end?
Appreciate the contributions of the different groups that worked to end slavery.
Understand the major milestones in the struggle for emancipation.
Identify the different groups that worked to end slavery.
Students would be able to:
6-HCT-K-1 Identify the groups that worked to end slavery. Pre -Assessment: Research - Are students able to define the following terms after independent research?

6-HCT-S-1 - Build a timeline of major milestones in the struggle for emancipation Timeline: Can students sequence the selected major milestones in the struggle for emancipation? The teacher should use an appropriate rubric to assess students' timelines.

6-HCT-V-1 Value the contribution of the enslaved people in resisting slavery and achieving emancipation. Creative Expression: Are students able to create items that reflect their appreciation of the contributions of enslaved Africans in resisting slavery and emancipation? Teachers use appropriate ru

6-HCT-K-2 Explain why East Indians were brought to the Caribbean. Product: Using information provided, can students identify the reasons why East Indians were brought to the Caribbean? See activity below. Raise Your Card - True/False: ● Students read aloud or listen to the passage ‘Mr. John's Journa

6-HCT-S-2 Chart the route taken by East Indian Indentured Servants from their homeland to the Caribbean. Product: Can students accurately chart the route taken by East Indian Indentured Servants from their homeland to the Caribbean? See activities below. Map Work- ● Students work in pairs to chart t

6-HCT-V-2 Appreciate the influences that East Indians have on Caribbean culture. Product: Do students' presentations identify at least three significant contributions of East Indian culture in the Caribbean? Are students able to explain how East Indians have positively influenced the culture of the

6-HCT-K-3 Describe common sport, culture and music activities that foster regional interactions. Conversation: Based on the focus question, are students able to give reasons why it is important for Caribbean people to work together?

6-HCT-S-3 Investigate local sport, culture or music figures that have influence beyond their home state Product: Research Small groups select one influential figure of their choice to conduct a research. This can be an internet search or an interview. Sample Interview Prompt/Research Guide 1. Name 2
6-HCT-V-3 Appreciate the value of arts and sports for shaping regional identity and pride.

6-HCT-K-4 Identify places where people from the Eastern Caribbean have migrated to. Observation: Are students able to analyse images depicting migration in the Caribbean, and provide oral descriptions?

6-HCT-S-4 - Locate where people of the Caribbean migrate. Product/Observation: With the use of handmade or digital stickers, can students identify the places where the people of the Caribbean migrate, on a blank map of the Caribbean/world map? Map work: Locating Places ● Using the Atlas, students lo
6-HCT-V-4 - Appreciate reasons why people migrate from their country and its impact on them and their home country.

essential_learning_outcomes.elo_description: To appreciate that the Caribbean has a diverse geography that poses challenges and opportunities
specific_curriculum_outcomes: Focus Questions:
Appreciate that the major regions each have unique features that help determine how people live there
Analyse the advantages and disadvantages of living in the various regions of the world
Describe how people in the various regions live (housing, clothing, agriculture, social life, transportation)
What physical features are unique to the various climatic zones?
How are climatic zones unique
What are the global climatic zones?
Focus Questions:
Appreciate that all regions of the world have unique natural features
Illustrate how other regions are alike or different from our region
tundra, forests, mountains, plains)
Identify the climatic regions of the world (tropical/torrid, temperate and polar/frigid) and describe their physical characteristics (e.g. deserts,
Grade 6 Social Studies Curriculum
Why are continents divided into countries?
Why is the Earth called the Blue Planet?
What features are seen on Earth from space?
Focus Questions:
Recognize that continents are made up of different countries.
Using a map or globe, locate the continents and major water bodies of the world in relation to the Caribbean region.
Name the continents and major water bodies of the world
6-ST-S-1 Locate the continents and major water bodies of the world on a map or globe
6-ST-V-1 Recognize that continents are made up of different countries.
6-ST-K-2 - Identify the climatic regions of the world (tropical/torrid, temperate and polar/frigid) and describe their physical characteristics (e.g. deserts, tundra, forests, mountains, plains )

6-ST-S-2 Illustrate how other regions are alike or different from our region Same or Different Observation Are students able to accurately identify their region on their map? Conversation Are students able to state the climatic zone that their region is located? Are students able to state that count
6-ST-V-2 -Appreciate that all regions of the world have unique natural features
6-ST-S-3 Analyze the advantages and disadvantages of living in the various regions of the world
6-ST-V-3 Appreciate that the major regions each have unique features that help determine how people live there

6-ST-K-4 Describe the extreme climatic conditions of these regions and explain how they are managed- (e.g. snow, windstorms, tsunamis). Weather News Report Conversation: Are students able to identify answer questions based on the information provided in the Weather News Report The lesson will begin

6-ST-S-4 Analyse the impact of extreme climatic conditions on people and their environment Analysing Case Studies Observation: Are students able to work collaboratively in groups to analyse the case study on the impact of severe climatic conditions?

6-ST-V-4 Appreciate that extreme climatic conditions have led to significant adaptations in the use of technology for securing food, shelter, clothing, and transportation Problem Solving Approach Observation Are students able to work collaboratively to identify possible solutions to severe climatic

essential_learning_outcomes.elo_description: To appreciate that complex global problems require international cooperation to make choices for the future
specific_curriculum_outcomes: What are the major social issues currently affecting the Eastern Caribbean, and how do they impact our daily lives?
Focus questions:
Appreciate that people have differing views on how to address social issues
Examine why these issues occur, how they can be solved, how they affect us in the Eastern Caribbean
Identify the major social issues in the Eastern Caribbean and the nearby world.
How can students appreciate the importance of international relations to their country's development?
How is the country connected to the different international organizations?
What is an international organisation?
Focus Questions:
Appreciate that cooperation is necessary for a country's Development
Examine the different international organizations to which their country belongs
Recognize that their country belongs to different international organisations
Grade 6 Social Studies Curriculum
How can citizens demonstrate pride for their national identity?
What key elements contribute to the national identity of a country?
What is the national identity?
Focus Questions:
Appreciate the diverse ways that citizens demonstrate pride for their national identity
Research an aspect of our national identity to show how it is influenced
Demonstrate understanding of factors which influence our national identity
6-CP-K-3 Identify the major social issues in the Eastern Caribbean and the nearby world.
6-CP-S-3 Examine why these issues occur, how they can be solved, how they affect us in the Eastern Caribbean

6-CP-V-3 Appreciate that people have differing views on how to address social issues. Product: Are students able to write and perform a short drama that illustrates the cause, effects, and solutions to a chosen issue.
6-CP-K-4 Discuss how different international organizations work to address social issues (WHO,FAO,IOM,UNICEF)
6-CP-S-4 S-Categorize the advantages and disadvantages of Product Are students able to write what meaning of the acronym for (WHO, FAO, IOM & UNICEF)?
6-CP-V-4 Appreciate the ways in which citizens of the Eastern Caribbean cooperate to do things for the country.
6-CP-K-5 Examine the role of citizens in the electoral process.
6-CP-S-5 Discuss the importance of participating in the electoral process as part of peoples' democratic rights.
6-CP-V-5 Value the importance of respecting person's democratic rights. Conversation Are students able to highlight how their vote influenced the outcome of the class vote?
6-CP-K-6 Explain the role of the media in shaping our views of local and international events
6-CP-S-6 Compare and analyze media sources from various countries to determine differing points of view about important events and issues
6-CP-V-6 Appreciate that as global citizens we have a responsibility to verify the accuracy of the media we consume and to engage responsibly in its use

essential_learning_outcomes.elo_description: Recognize that regional and international movement of goods, people, and services shapes Caribbean society
specific_curriculum_outcomes: How do positive international relations benefit countries and individuals?
Focus Questions:
Appreciate the importance of maintaining positive relations with others in the world
Investigate the negative consequences of poor international relations with other countries
Recognize the importance of good relations with the wider world
How can communities benefit from tourism without losing their identity or traditions?
How can tourism bring both positive and negative changes to our community?
What are some ways that tourism helps economic growth?
Focus Questions:
Appreciate that there are positive and negative effects of tourism on our country
Critically assess the opportunities and challenges that tourism faces in our country
State the benefits derived from tourism for our country
Grade 6 Social Studies Curriculum
How can governments and organisations protect consumer rights?
What is the difference between consumer rights and responsibilities?
What are the basic rights of consumers?
Focus Questions:
Appreciate the value of making responsible decisions as consumers for shaping our economic and social well-being
Plan and develop budgets to guide our consumption
Know that as consumers we have rights and responsibilities
6-EDM-S-1 Plan and develop budgets to guide our consumption Can students explain the kinds of information found on receipts and utility bills?

6-EDM-V-1 Appreciate the value of making responsible decisions as consumers for shaping our economic and social well-being Can students identify practices that demonstrate consumers applying their rights? Can students differentiate between consumer rights and consumer responsibilities? What is the d
6-EDM-K-2 state the benefits derived from tourism for our country
6-EDM-S-2 Critically assess the opportunities and challenges that tourism faces in our country

6-EDM-V-2 Appreciate that there are positive and negative effects of tourism on our country What are the economic benefits of tourism for the Caribbean region Do some islands benefit more than through tourism? Can students highlight the measures taken by the Government to protect natural and cultura
6-EDM-K-3 Recognize the importance of good relations with the wider world

6-EDM-S-3 Investigate the negative consequences of poor international relations with other countries Product: Can students gain an understanding of the impact of poor international relations by analyzing real-world conflicts? Case Study Exploration Steps: 1. Provide students with a digital case stud

6-EDM-V-3 Appreciate the importance of maintaining positive relations with others in the world Product: Can students reflect on the value of positive international relations and their personal role in promoting them?
